coreIssue: This bi-weekly check-in is likely about how Carla is handling competing scope between leading AZPocket design delivery and the global CoE.
openingQuestion: What tradeoffs are you making right now between AZPocket and the CoE, and where do you most need clearer backing from me?
listenFor: whether they name one concrete task, meeting, or decision they are actively delaying because of the split scope
whether they volunteer a specific stakeholder or forum where priorities are being framed differently across AZPocket and the CoE
if they stay high-level instead of naming what they have said no to, paused, or escalated in the last two weeks
avoid: do not treat this like a personal time-management problem if the real issue is unclear lead-level scope or competing expectations
do not jump into solving both lanes at once before you know which decisions or dependencies are actually stuck
goodOutcome: You and Carla have agreed which lead-level scope she owns end-to-end over the next two weeks, and which AZPocket or CoE work is explicitly deprioritized or escalated.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example where AZPocket delivery and CoE leadership appeared to compete so you can ground the conversation in a real tradeoff.
